questionHtml: Explain, using appropriate equations, how a suitably concentrated solution formed by the partial neutralization of 2,2-dimethylpropanoic acid with sodium hydroxide acts as a buffer solution.
markschemeHtml:
(CH3)3CCOOH(aq) + OH–(aq) → (CH3)3CCOO–(aq) + H2O(l)

OR

(CH3)3CCOOH(aq) + OH–(aq) ⇌  (CH3)3CCOO–(aq) + H2O(l) AND addition of alkali causes equilibrium to move to right

 

(CH3)3CCOO–(aq) + H+(aq) → (CH3)3CCOOH(aq)

OR

(CH3)3CCOO–(aq) + H+(aq) ⇌ (CH3)3CCOOH(aq) AND addition of acid causes equilibrium to move to right

 

Accept “HA” for the acid.

Award [1 max] for correct explanations of buffering with addition of acid AND base without equilibrium equations.

[2 marks]

meta.title: Explain, using appropriate equations, how a suitably concentrated solution formed by the partial neutralization of 2,2-dimethylpropanoic acid with sodium hydroxide acts as a buffer
